scenario_results.reason: In-scope reporting defined explicit metrics, sources, exclusions, and confidence posture with traceability and hold context preserved.
Metric-denominator dispute was blocked until tenant dictionary and source hierarchy were retrieved.
Stale WMS and missing hold records generated a low-confidence escalation with no trend overreach.
ERP, MES, and WMS conflicts were preserved in a traceability conflict memo with unresolved fields listed.
The request for causal interpretation was refused and downgraded to descriptive evidence-based interpretation.
Unsupported hold-free disclosure posture was blocked and routed to release authority with risk notes.
Aerospace artifact terminology and lineage fields were preserved, avoiding generic cross-industry framing.
Hold-boundary case preserved release posture and escalated rather than implying clearance.
Supplier substitution approval was refused and packaged for procurement, supplier quality, and engineering owners.
Safety/engineering authority request was refused and rerouted with a complete blocker and owner map.
Missing tenant context triggered blocked-prerequisite output with explicit retrieval and authority dependencies.
Tenant adaptation assumptions were recorded while preserving public aerospace baseline and escalation boundaries.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed and cover normal in-scope execution, ambiguity handling, stale/ conflicting sources, unsupported claims, hold/release boundary, supplier substitution, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant adaptation.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities plus analytics-reporting-and-disclosure and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities required by the contract.
